text.title: gov-license-compliance-scan Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for gov-license-compliance-scan. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every repo MUST commit a `license-policy.yml` (or equivalent FOSSA / Black Duck policy) listing `allow`, `flag`, and `deny` SPDX identifiers per build target. The CI MUST fail when any dependency resolves to a `deny` license OR introduces a license not in `allow` / `flag`. For proprietary SaaS the deny list MUST include at minimum `GPL-2.0-only`, `GPL-3.0-only`, `AGPL-3.0-only`, `SSPL-1.0`. The agent MUST refuse to add a dependency whose declared license is not on the allowlist; if uncertain, escalate to a human, never silently accept. On every dependency change the agent MUST regenerate `NOTICE` / `THIRD_PARTY.md` from the lockfile, not by hand.
rule.rationale: Encoding the policy as machine-readable data turns license review from a once-a-quarter human task into a per-PR build gate; the agent gets a deterministic boundary instead of having to "decide" what is acceptable.

rule.statement: Every tagged release MUST produce a CycloneDX 1.6 OR SPDX 2.3 SBOM regenerated from the lockfile (`pnpm-lock.yaml`, `uv.lock`, `Cargo.lock`, `go.sum`, `pom.xml`, etc.). The SBOM MUST be signed with `cosign attest` (keyless OIDC or KMS) and attached to the GitHub Release / artifact registry. The SBOM MUST be regenerated — NEVER hand-edited. The CI MUST fail the release if the SBOM is missing or its signature does not verify. Older SBOMs MUST be retained for the regulatory window (EU CRA: 10 years from end-of-support).
rule.rationale: A signed SBOM is the only evidence an auditor can trust; a hand-edited or unsigned SBOM is unfalsifiable and provides no compliance value when a CVE is reported against a transitive dep.
